channel.title: +Alani on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Alani on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Religion
item.description: Phases in Ossetia An original Scythian Paganism; a first wave of Christianisation under Byzantine and Georgian influence from the tenth to the thirteenth century (partial elite conversion); a “re-Paganization” during the fourteenth and fifteenth century following the Mongol invasions and the disruption of the contacts with the Byzantines; a partial re-Christianisation during the sixteenth and seventeenth century conducted by Georgian missionaries; a further re-Christianisation conducted by Russian missionaries beginning in the late eighteenth century; enforced state atheism during the Soviet Union from 1921 to 1991; and a resurgence of “traditional Ossetian religion” since the 1980s–1990s.
